site.title: NRL TipBot Briefing 2026
site.subhead: Round 12 wrap board for tipping, charity betting, and weekly NRL intel
site.intro: A weekly NRL command board for sharper tipping, cleaner bet tracking, and banking any wins for charity.

meta.description: Codex-managed NRL TipBot dashboard for Round 12 results, charity betting discipline, and weekly NRL intel.

ticker: round 12 landed as a 5 from 5 tip sweep across the shortened origin slate
dolphins beat raiders 30 to 22 after absorbing a heavy second-half push
bulldogs beat storm 30 to 20 and cashed the cleanest roster-depletion read of the week
warriors handled dragons 30 to 12 while sea eagles held off titans 12 to 10
cowboys closed the round with a 30 to 18 win over rabbitohs
the mark hughes foundation ledger still sits at 2 and 2 and minus $3.50 because no round 12 charity bet was locked
validated read: keep taking clean team-news edges without forcing a bet

featurePanels.title: Round Read
items: Round 12 finished 5 from 5 on tips across the reduced Origin-week slate
The Bulldogs roster edge over Melbourne converted exactly as expected
The Dolphins, Warriors, Sea Eagles and Cowboys all held their cleaner pre-game reads
This validates the simple team-news process, not a reason to get clever next week

featurePanels.title: Risk Flags
items: Sea Eagles versus Titans was as tight as flagged, with Manly only surviving 12-10
The Dolphins had to defend hard late after the Raiders kept finding chances
A clean tips week can create false confidence if it turns into stake creep
No Round 12 charity bet was placed, so do not rewrite the ledger as a betting win

featurePanels.title: Weekly Focus
items: Carry forward the cleaner-team-news-first approach into the next slate
Separate tip confidence from charity-bet discipline every week
Respect the Warriors' floor while the Dragons remain winless
Treat Manly's narrow win as a warning that disrupted line-ups still create sweat

featurePanels.title: Watchlist
items: Dolphins depth held despite the Queensland Origin absences
Bulldogs can punish depleted elite teams when their own structure is intact
Warriors continue to look like a high-floor side against weaker opponents
Cowboys did enough at home once South Sydney's missing stars lowered the ceiling

betCard.title: Round 12 Charity Bet
betCard.pick: No Round 12 charity play was locked
why: The Bulldogs shortlist read won, but it was never converted into a locked charity bet.
Holding the stake on a messy Origin slate was still consistent with the process.
The result is a tips validation, while the charity ledger remains unchanged.
risks: Do not retrospectively count an unplaced Bulldogs lean as a charity result.
The clean sweep should not push the next round into forced betting.
Any Round 13 charity play still needs a fresh price and team-news edge.

charity.title: Mark Hughes Foundation Ledger
charity.summary: All profits are earmarked for the Mark Hughes Foundation.
notes: Round 5: Newcastle head to head at $2.30 won and collected $11.50.
Round 6: North Queensland Cowboys head to head at $2.00 won and collected $10.00.
Round 7: Wests Tigers head to head at $1.53 lost in a 21-20 defeat to Brisbane.
Round 8: No charity bet placed, bank held at $6.50.
Round 9: Bulldogs head to head at $1.77 lost after a Cowboys comeback win.
Rounds 10 and 11: No charity bet locked, so the ledger stayed unchanged.
Round 12: No charity bet locked; tips went 5 from 5, but the ledger stayed unchanged.

watchlist: Do not let the 5 from 5 tips week inflate Round 13 stake sizing.
Keep Dolphins depth upgraded after they handled the Raiders without key Origin names.
Keep the Warriors upgraded while the Dragons remain winless.
Review Round 13 team lists before moving any charity bet from shortlist to locked.

sections.title: Situation Report
sections.heading: Round 12 finished as a clean sweep, but the process stays boring
lines: As of Monday, 25 May 2026, official NRL results have been checked and the Round 12 tip card went 5 from 5.
Dolphins, Bulldogs, Warriors, Sea Eagles and Cowboys all won, with Manly's 12-10 escape the only real danger point.
The charity ledger is still parked at 2-2 and -$3.50 because no Round 12 bet was actually locked.

sections.title: Bet Analysis
sections.heading: Bulldogs won the shortlist read, but the ledger does not move
lines: The Bulldogs beat the Storm 30-20, so the main roster-depletion read was correct.
Because the charity bet was never locked, the Mark Hughes Foundation record remains 2-2 and -$3.50.
This is the right accounting: a good read can be logged as validation without inventing a betting result.
Round 13 still needs a fresh price check before any stake goes live.

sections.title: Round 12 Tips
sections.heading: Origin-week tip card after full-time
lines: Dolphins over Raiders - won 30-22.
Bulldogs over Storm - won 30-20.
Warriors over Dragons - won 30-12.
Sea Eagles over Titans - won 12-10.
Cowboys over Rabbitohs - won 30-18.

sections.title: Round 12 Fixtures
sections.heading: Reduced Round 12 slate results
lines: Raiders 22, Dolphins 30.
Bulldogs 30, Storm 20.
Dragons 12, Warriors 30.
Sea Eagles 12, Titans 10.
Cowboys 30, Rabbitohs 18.

sections.title: Form Radar
sections.heading: Who deserves the upgrade or downgrade after Round 12
lines: Hot: Warriors, Dolphins depth, Bulldogs in this specific matchup, and the Cowboys at home.
Cold: Dragons, Storm's round-specific depth, and the Rabbitohs' attacking ceiling without Latrell and Campbell Graham.
The trap warning on Sea Eagles versus Titans was justified; Manly won, but only by two points.

sections.title: Intel Notes
sections.heading: The data points validated by the final scores
lines: The Dolphins still won without their Queensland Origin stars and can keep a system-depth upgrade.
Kiraz back in for Canterbury and the Storm's Origin holes were the cleanest read of the week.
The Warriors' structure was far too much for a winless Dragons side.
South Sydney's missing stars mattered enough for the Cowboys to control the Sunday result.
